project_overview.project_name: Lebanon: Green Agri-food transformation for economic recovery (GATE)
project_overview.project_development_objective: Improve the resilience of farmers and small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) operating in Lebanon's agri-food sector
project_overview.beneficiaries: Approximately 80,000 farming families, small and medium-sized enterprises in agri-food sector, rural communities, women, youth, and displaced populations

geographic_scope.coverage_type: national with focus on rural areas
geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: yes - Lebanon is experiencing unprecedented economic and financial crisis, institutional paralysis, and security challenges affecting rural agricultural communities

key_components.component_name: Component 1: Climate-smart investments in agri-food value chains
key_components.description: Support for farmer groups and SMEs through matching grants for climate-smart business plans, technical assistance, and credit facilities for small and medium enterprises in agri-food sector

key_components.component_name: Component 2: Climate-smart infrastructure and services for agri-food sector development
key_components.description: Rehabilitation of rural infrastructure including irrigation systems, water storage (hill lakes), rural roads, wastewater treatment, solid waste management, and farmer market centers

key_components.component_name: Component 3: Improving enabling environment and restoring support services
key_components.description: Support for climate-smart agricultural practices, food safety systems, agricultural extension services, digitalization, and export promotion
Project coordination, monitoring and evaluation, environmental and social standards compliance, grievance mechanisms, and climate benefit reporting
Unfunded component for emergency response to unforeseen crises

implementation_arrangements.implementing_agency: Council for Development and Reconstruction (CDR) through Project Coordination Unit (PCU)
implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: Steering Committee chaired by Ministers of Agriculture, Energy and Water, and Economy and Trade; Technical evaluation committees; Coordination with municipalities, municipal unions, and sectoral ministries (Environment, Energy and Water, Agriculture); Partnerships with Kafalat (guarantee company for SME lending), Green Project (for rural infrastructure), and sectoral institutions
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: World Bank Procurement Regulations (November 2020); Standard bidding documents; Direct selection for consultants; Commercial practices for private sector beneficiaries; Competitive selection for farmer groups and SMEs
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: Quarterly monitoring reports; Annual financial audits by independent external auditor; Third-party monitoring agency; Technical audits for infrastructure; Beneficiary satisfaction surveys; Gender-disaggregated indicators; Climate benefit tracking; Grievance mechanism with hotline; Real-time monitoring using KoBoToolbox and GIS systems

identified_risks.risk_description: Lebanon has been without a president since October 2022; caretaker government with limited executive authority; political paralysis impeding structural reforms and decision-making on critical reforms
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Close monitoring of political context; Engagement with authorities at highest levels; Coordination with development partners; Technical assistance for financial sector reforms; Flexibility in implementation arrangements

identified_risks.risk_description: Unprecedented economic and financial crisis; currency devaluation; three-digit inflation; banking sector insolvency; multiple exchange rates; high uncertainty regarding economic stabilization
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: All project disbursements in USD; Beneficiary payments in USD; Unification of exchange rate as IMF precondition; World Bank technical assistance for financial sector reforms; Flexible implementation timelines

identified_risks.risk_description: Weak institutional capacity of implementing agencies; Loss of qualified staff due to salary erosion and currency fluctuations; Limited experience of some implementing partners (e.g., Kafalat in direct lending); Capacity constraints in public sector
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Strong technical assistance and capacity building; Hiring experienced staff; Training programs; Detailed operational manuals; Close World Bank supervision; Support for institutional strengthening; Phased implementation with performance assessments

identified_risks.risk_description: High corruption perception in Lebanon; Complex implementation involving multiple agencies and beneficiaries; Limited capacity of State Audit Office; Fiduciary risks rated high overall
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Third-party monitoring agents; Independent external auditors; Technical audits; Transparent selection criteria; Detailed operational manuals; Grievance mechanisms; Regular World Bank supervision; Fraud and corruption guidelines compliance

identified_risks.risk_description: Complex multi-sectoral project design; Multiple implementing agencies requiring coordination; Limited experience of some partners; Risks in SME lending component (foreign exchange exposure, non-repayment, operational risks)
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Experienced implementing partners; Technical assistance and capacity building; Detailed operational manuals; Phased implementation; Performance assessments; First five loan agreements subject to World Bank review; Careful beneficiary selection; Monitoring of portfolio quality

identified_risks.risk_description: Ongoing security challenges in Lebanon; Potential for civil unrest; Risks to project staff and beneficiaries in certain areas
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Security risk assessments; Flexible implementation arrangements; Community engagement; Conflict-sensitive approach; Grievance mechanisms

identified_risks.risk_description: Risks related to child labor in agriculture; Gender-based violence; Exclusion of vulnerable groups; Land acquisition for infrastructure; Water resource management; Waste management; Pesticide use
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Environmental and Social Framework compliance; Environmental and Social Commitment Plan; Labor management procedures; Child labor prevention program with NGO service provider; Grievance mechanisms; Gender-responsive design; Stakeholder engagement; Environmental assessments; Safeguard policies triggered

identified_risks.risk_description: Risks related to foreign exchange volatility; Multiple exchange rates; Potential capital controls; Beneficiary access to funds; Sustainability of operations and maintenance
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: All disbursements in USD; Beneficiary payments in USD; Realistic O&M plans; Capacity building for O&M; Sustainable financing mechanisms; Phased implementation

triggered_safeguard_policies: International Waterways (OP/BP 7.50) - exemption granted for small-scale rehabilitation

Environmental and Social Framework (ESF) - Labor and Working Conditions (ESS2), Resource Efficiency and Pollution Prevention (ESS3), Community Health and Safety (ESS4), Land Acquisition and Involuntary Resettlement (ESS5), Stakeholder Engagement and Information Disclosure (ESS10)
safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Project-level grievance mechanism with hotline for receiving complaints and reports; Referral pathways for child labor and gender-based violence issues; Complaints registered in grievance log; Response within 15 days; Appeals process; Coordination with third-party monitoring; Regular reporting to World Bank within 48 hours for SEA/SH cases
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Significant gender gaps identified: women own only 9% of farms and cultivate 5% of farmland; receive only 3% of bank loans despite owning 30% of small enterprises; limited access to productive resources, training, markets, and social protection; concentrated in subsistence farming and post-harvest activities. Project design includes: reduced matching grant contributions for women (10% vs 20% for others); women-focused communication campaigns; gender-disaggregated training; support for women-led SMEs; women's participation in decision-making; gender specialist in PCU; gender-disaggregated monitoring indicators; support for women's access to markets and export opportunities
vulnerable_groups: Displaced populations (Syrian refugees and Lebanese IDPs)

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project designed with conflict-sensitive approach; Recognizes Lebanon's fragility and conflict-affected context; Aims to build resilience and trust in government services; Inclusive stakeholder engagement; Grievance mechanisms to prevent tensions; Focus on livelihood opportunities to reduce vulnerability; Community-driven project selection; Participatory planning processes; Attention to equitable resource distribution across regions
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Lebanon experiencing unprecedented economic and financial crisis described as among worst globally since 1950s; Banking sector insolvency and paralysis; Currency collapse and three-digit inflation; Institutional dysfunction with political vacuum (no president since October 2022); Caretaker government with limited authority; High poverty and food insecurity; Collapse of public services; Multiple overlapping crises (economic, political, security); Large displaced population (Syrian refugees and Lebanese IDPs); Weak institutional capacity; High unemployment and limited livelihood opportunities
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Project acknowledges security challenges in Lebanon; Implementation arrangements include flexibility for security risks; Community engagement and conflict-sensitive approach; Grievance mechanisms to address tensions; Focus on building trust and social cohesion through livelihood support; Monitoring of security context; Potential risks to project staff and beneficiaries in certain areas; Project designed to support stability and recovery
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - Project explicitly targets displaced populations including Syrian refugees and Lebanese IDPs as vulnerable beneficiary groups; Recognizes high prevalence of child labor among displaced Syrian families (4.4% in 2020 vs 2.6% in 2019); Aims to provide livelihood opportunities and economic empowerment to displaced populations; Includes specific measures to prevent child labor exploitation among displaced children; Gender-based violence monitoring includes displaced populations; Project designed to support economic recovery and resilience for all vulnerable groups including displaced persons
